title: Tipi
desc: Painted Lodge Buffalo Hide, 14 Lodge Poles; Accession Year: 1893; Accession Number: 46; Collected by: Isaac Cowie; Collection Site: North America, Canada, Alberta, Edmonton; Courtesy of Chicago Field Museum

title: Tipi Cover, Door, and Pegs
desc: Canvas tipi <REG: tepee> and door with painted decoration and bundle of nine pegs decorated with feathers, and bundle of pegs, used for holding tipi flap. Formerly belonged to Dusty Horn, medicine man of the Poor Man Reservation. Materials: Cotton canvas, Wood, Feather(s), Paint; Techniques: Sewn, Painted, Carved; Acquisition Source: Cadzow, Donald A. (Collector); Cadzow, Donald A. (MAI Agent); Photos by Walter Larrimore; Collection Site: File Hills Reserve; Courtesy of the Smithsonian: National Museum of the American Indian

title: Buffalo Bull and Family In Front of Painted Tipi
desc: Group ("Buffalo Bull" and family) in costume in front of painted tipi. Prairie Cree. File Hills Reservation. Saskatchewan. Canada. Photo by D. A. Cadzow, 1926.NMAI Archives Collection #NMAI.AC.001.004. Courtesy of the Smithsonian: National Museum of the American Indian

title: Painted Tipi (Image Not Displayed)
desc: Painted tipi. Used for Council Meetings. Cree Tribe. File Hills Reservation. Saskatchewan. Canada. 1926. Presented by Kenneth C. Miller. NMAI Archives Collection #NMAI.AC.001.049. Courtesy of the Smithsonian: National Museum of the American Indian

title: Woman and Painted Tipi (Image Not Displayed)
desc: Woman and painted tipi (Buffalo Bull's tipi). Cree Tribe. File Hills Reservation. Saskatchewan. Canada. 1926. Presented by Kenneth C. Miller. NMAI Archives Collection #NMAI.AC.001.049. Courtesy of the Smithsonian: National Museum of the American Indian

title: Painted Council Tipi (Image Not Displayed)
desc: Painted Council tipi. Cree Tribe. File Hills Reservation.Saskatchewan. Canada. 1926. Presented by Kenneth C. Miller. NMAI Archives Collection #NMAI.AC.001.049. Courtesy of the Smithsonian: National Museum of the American Indian
